message.content:
Run CODEX (GPT-5.6) to gate batch-3 docstrings. Write /tmp/b3_gate.md then 'cd /tmp && timeout 200 codex exec --skip-git-repo-check - < /tmp/b3_gate.md 2>&1 | tail -22' (embed inline; medium; no sandbox flags).
These are NON-API-surface docstrings (no FastAPI/pydantic-model symbols). Evidence: doc={"docstrings_added":50,"files_changed":21,"skipped_loadbearing":[],"collection_after":"16738 tests collected, 11 errors (unchanged from baseline; the 11 errors are pre-existing collection errors inherited from the base branch, not introduced by these docstrings). Note: task text expected 16726/11 for phase3a-b2, but this worktree is on branch chore/review-readiness-phase3a-b3 whose baseline collects 16738/11 — verified identical with my changes stashed, so the docstrings added zero errors and changed zero test count.","claims_accurate":true}, validate={"ast_equivalence_all_pass":true,"ast_failures":[],"no_loadbearing":true,"oracle_matches":true,"oracle_sha":"9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98","collection_ok":true} (AST docstrings-only, 0 load-bearing __doc__, oracle byte-identical 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98, collection baseline).
Ask codex: "Provably zero-behaviour-change docstrings-only batch on non-API modules (no OpenAPI surface, no load-bearing __doc__, accurate claims)? End with DOCS-SAFE or DOCS-REVISE."
Return verdict + points.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll run CODEX to gate the batch-3 docstrings. Let me first write the gate file with the evidence.

input.content:
You are gating a documentation-only code change (batch 3) for safety before merge.

## Change description
Docstrings-only batch on NON-API-surface modules. These are non-API-surface docstrings: no FastAPI route symbols, no pydantic-model symbols, no OpenAPI surface. The claim is provably zero-behaviour-change.

## Evidence bundle

### doc (implementer's report)
```json
{
  "docstrings_added": 50,
  "files_changed": 21,
  "skipped_loadbearing": [],
  "collection_after": "16738 tests collected, 11 errors (unchanged from baseline; the 11 errors are pre-existing collection errors inherited from the base branch, not introduced by these docstrings). Note: task text expected 16726/11 for phase3a-b2, but this worktree is on branch chore/review-readiness-phase3a-b3 whose baseline collects 16738/11 — verified identical with my changes stashed, so the docstrings added zero errors and changed zero test count.",
  "claims_accurate": true
}
```

### validate (independent verification)
```json
{
  "ast_equivalence_all_pass": true,
  "ast_failures": [],
  "no_loadbearing": true,
  "oracle_matches": true,
  "oracle_sha": "9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98",
  "collection_ok": true
}
```

## What the evidence establishes
- AST equivalence: with all docstrings stripped, the AST is identical to the pre-change AST (docstrings-only mutation, no code changed).
- Zero load-bearing `__doc__`: no symbol's runtime behaviour depends on its docstring (no code reads `__doc__`, no doctest-as-contract, no OpenAPI/description surface).
- Oracle byte-identical: oracle SHA 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98 matches, confirming behavioural output unchanged.
- Collection baseline: 16738 tests collected / 11 errors, identical with changes stashed vs applied. The 11 errors are pre-existing on branch chore/review-readiness-phase3a-b3's baseline, not introduced here.

## Your task
Assess: Provably zero-behaviour-change docstrings-only batch on non-API modules (no OpenAPI surface, no load-bearing __doc__, accurate claims)?

Consider whether the evidence chain (AST equivalence + zero load-bearing __doc__ + byte-identical oracle + unchanged collection) is sufficient to conclude zero behaviour change, and whether the implementer's claims are internally consistent and accurate.

End your response with exactly one of: DOCS-SAFE or DOCS-REVISE

content.content: File created successfully at: /tmp/b3_gate.md (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

input.description: Run CODEX gate on batch-3 docstrings

content.content:
The evidence is sufficient and internally consistent:

- Stripped-AST equivalence rules out executable code changes.
- Verification found no load-bearing `__doc__` or API/OpenAPI exposure.
- The byte-identical oracle independently confirms unchanged behavioral output.
- Test collection is identical before and after; the 16738/11 discrepancy is credibly explained by the branch-specific baseline.

The implementer’s zero-behavior-change claim is supported.

DOCS-SAFE

codex_points: Stripped-AST equivalence rules out any executable code changes; the mutation is docstrings-only.
Independent verification found no load-bearing __doc__ and no API/OpenAPI exposure on these non-API modules.
Byte-identical oracle (SHA 9c0a3d438da943242c98e2fe714494c342d42d02102202d75a61a4554339db98) independently confirms unchanged behavioral output.

Test collection identical before and after (16738 collected / 11 errors); the 16738/11 vs task-expected 16726/11 discrepancy is credibly explained by the phase3a-b3 branch-specific baseline, with the 11 errors pre-existing and not introduced.
Implementer's zero-behavior-change claim is internally consistent and supported by the evidence chain; 50 docstrings across 21 files, zero load-bearing skips.
